required_fields.description: A concise human-readable summary of the change in imperative mood. Maximum 120 characters. Must describe what the change does, not how it was implemented.
The classification of this change. Must match the change_class assigned in the task contract and must be one of the standard change_class enum values.
The version impact of this change as determined by the architect and confirmed by the release manager. Must conform to versioning-rules.json change_type_mapping.
A detailed description of what changed and why. Must be specific enough for a consumer to understand the impact without reading the diff. Minimum 30 characters.
The module, component, layer, or subsystem affected by this change. Used for filtering and release note grouping. Must reference a recognized area within the project.
A reference to the task contract, issue number, or pull request that authorized this change.
The approval class used for the change. Must match the task contract approval_class.

optional_fields.description: Whether this change breaks backward compatibility. Must be true when change_class is breaking-change. Must be true when version_impact is major and the change alters public API, policy, schema, default behavior, or consumer obligations.
optional_fields.conditional_requirement: Required and must be true when change_class is breaking-change or version_impact is major.

optional_fields.description: Instructions for consumers on how to migrate from the previous behavior to the new behavior. Required when breaking_change is true. Must be actionable and specific.
Expanded migration guidance for breaking changes. Required when breaking_change is true if the entry is rendered in Markdown with a dedicated Migration Guidance field.
Downstream consumers, schemas, workflows, validators, documentation surfaces, or integrations that must adapt to the change.

change_class_requirements.description: Defines which change_class values require a changelog entry and which are exempt.

requires_entry.reason: All new features must be documented in the changelog for consumer awareness.
All bug fixes must be documented so consumers know which defects were corrected.
All breaking changes must be prominently documented with migration notes and affected consumers.
All governance changes must be documented for transparency and auditability.
Release process changes that affect version impact must be documented.

entry_conditional.condition: Required only when version_impact is not none. Internal refactors with no external impact are exempt.

exempt.condition: Exempt when version_impact is none. Required if documentation change affects user-facing behavior descriptions.
Exempt when version_impact is none. Required if metadata change affects build output or artifact content.

constraints: migration_note must be present and actionable.
migration_guidance must be present and actionable when the entry format supports it.
affected_consumers must identify downstream consumers that must adapt.
summary must explicitly state what breaks and for whom.
title must indicate the breaking nature of the change.

immutability_policy.description: The changelog is a governance record. Released history must not be rewritten by normal feature, bugfix, docs, or governance work.
blocked_mutations: removing migration guidance from a released breaking change
moving a released entry to hide original release context

required_consistency: released sections have no deleted or rewritten lines

validation_rules.description: All required fields must be present and non-empty in every changelog entry.
change_class, version_impact, and approval_class must contain values from their respective enum definitions.
version_impact in the changelog entry must match the release impact in the task contract and the change_type_mapping in versioning-rules.json.
When breaking_change is true, migration fields and affected consumers must be present. When change_class is breaking-change, breaking_change must be true.
New entries must be added under Unreleased and released changelog history must not be silently rewritten.

rejection_conditions.description: Any required field is absent or empty.
change_class, version_impact, or approval_class contains a value not in the standard enum.
breaking_change is true or required but migration fields or affected consumers are missing.
Diff evidence shows a breaking change but breaking_change is false or change_class is not breaking-change.
version_impact in changelog does not match the task contract or violates change_type_mapping.
A change_class that requires an entry has no corresponding changelog entry.
A new changelog entry is not located under the Unreleased section.
Released changelog history is deleted or rewritten outside a dedicated correction contract.
